Title: Discover the maze-like network for glabridin biosynthesis

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: GAL2 promoter in yeast functional assays
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reductively cleaves the dihydrofuran ring of pterocarpans, converting medicarpin to vestitol and demethylmedicarpin to demethylvestitol
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies immediate precursor pool for prenylation and downstream glabridin formation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antioxidant flavonoid with skin-brightening, anti-inflammatory, and anti-aging properties
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes glabridin biosynthesis through efficient conversion of medicarpin into vestitol in the dominant PTR-PT-OC route
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GgPTR1 efficiently converted medicarpin and demethylmedicarpin to vestitol and demethylvestitol in yeast and in vitro. 2. It was prioritized by co-expression and showed stronger activity than other PTR homologs. 3. When combined with GgPT1, GgOC1, and DMTs, it enabled glabridin production in reconstructed yeast pathways.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Co-expression prioritization; heterologous expression in yeast feeding assays; purified enzyme in vitro assay; structural modeling
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Galactose induction in yeast assays; substrate feeding
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Strong conversion to vestitol can outcompete alternative branches; extracellular leakage of downstream demethylated intermediates contributes to lower yield in single-route design
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful core tailoring enzyme for microbial glabridin biosynthesis; should be stacked with GgPT1, GgOC1 and moderated demethylation to preserve multi-route robustness
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: GgPTR1 showed no activity toward prenylated substrate licoagrocarpin (20), unlike GgPTR2
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq: NCBI BioProject PRJNA1390709; metabolomics: MetaboLights MTBLS13519

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in stems and leaves
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: GAL2 promoter in yeast functional assays
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reductively cleaves the dihydrofuran ring of medicarpin and demethylmedicarpin, and also reduces prenylated licoagrocarpin to 4′-O-methylpreglabridin
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms alternative route intermediates leading to glabridin
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antioxidant flavonoid with skin-brightening, anti-inflammatory, and anti-aging properties
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports an alternative PT-PTR-OC route to glabridin and recognizes prenylated substrates selectively
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GgPTR2 had moderate activity toward medicarpin and low activity toward demethylmedicarpin. 2. Unlike GgPTR1, it converted licoagrocarpin to 4′-O-methylpreglabridin. 3. Its promiscuity enabled side-route formation in reconstructed yeast pathways.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Yeast feeding assays; purified enzyme in vitro assay; structural modeling
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Galactose induction in yeast assays; substrate feeding
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Promiscuity generated side products such as 9 and 13 in PT-PTR-OC pathway reconstruction
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for alternative route engineering but may increase pathway complexity and by-products
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Distinct substrate-binding orientation explains acceptance of prenylated substrate 20
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq: NCBI BioProject PRJNA1390709; metabolomics: MetaboLights MTBLS13519

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: UbiA-type aromatic prenyltransferase; multi-pass membrane protein
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Higher transcript level in G. glabra roots than in G. inflata and G. uralensis; root-enriched
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: GAL2 promoter in yeast functional assays; constitutive P_THD3 in localization assay
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Prenylates isoflavan substrates vestitol and demethylvestitol to form 4′-O-methylpreglabridin and preglabridin
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate prenylated precursors for oxidative cyclization to glabridin
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antioxidant flavonoid with skin-brightening, anti-inflammatory, and anti-aging properties
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Essentially promotes glabridin accumulation by introducing the prenyl group required for downstream cyclization
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Among seven PT candidates, only GgPT1 prenylated vestitol and demethylvestitol. 2. K242 was essential for catalytic activity. 3. High PT transcript abundance in G. glabra helps explain species-specific glabridin accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Co-expression prioritization; yeast feeding assays; mutational analysis; subcellular localization
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Important control point for species-specific accumulation, but not the main rate-limiting step in engineered yeast
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Galactose induction in yeast assays; DMAPP supply engineering affected interpretation of prenylation efficiency
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Low prenyl donor supply limited flux; in vitro microsome reconstitution failed, likely due to membrane dependence
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value enzyme for glabridin pathway reconstruction; should be combined with DMAPP supply engineering and downstream OC optimization
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Did not convert medicarpin to licoagrocarpin; PcM4DT was used for that alternative prenylation step
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq: NCBI BioProject PRJNA1390709; metabolomics: MetaboLights MTBLS13519

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Root-expressed; higher transcript level in G. glabra than in G. inflata; asymmetric co-expression with upstream genes
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: GAL2 promoter in yeast functional assays; constitutive promoter in localization assay
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Oxidative cyclization branch of tailoring module
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes oxidative cyclization of prenylated isoflavans to form the dihydrobenzopyran-containing products 4′-O-methylglabridin and glabridin
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms glabridin or its immediate methylated precursor
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antioxidant flavonoid with skin-brightening, anti-inflammatory, and anti-aging properties
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Directly enables terminal cyclization to glabridin and is the major bottleneck in the engineered pathway
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Only GgOC1, not GgOC2 or other tested BBE-like enzymes, catalyzed oxidative cyclization to 11 and 15. 2. In vitro microsome assays confirmed its function. 3. The oxidative cyclization step emerged as the rate-limiting step for glabridin biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Co-expression prioritization; yeast feeding assays; microsomal in vitro assay; mutational analysis; phylogenetic analysis; subcellular localization
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Rate-limiting enzyme in engineered glabridin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Galactose induction in yeast assays; membrane environment important for activity
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Low catalytic efficiency caused accumulation of upstream intermediate 14
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Top target for protein engineering to improve glabridin titer; should be optimized together with membrane context
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Residues I293 and T295 are essential for catalysis
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq: NCBI BioProject PRJNA1390709; metabolomics: MetaboLights MTBLS13519

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Root-associated; seasonal expression opposite to O-methyltransferase expression; higher transcript level in G. glabra than in G. inflata
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: GAL2 promoter in yeast functional assays; cyanamide-inducible DDI2 promoter in de novo pathway
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Demethylates 4′-O-methylated isoflavonoid intermediates, including 4′-O-methylglabridin to glabridin, and also acts on vestitol and 4′-O-methylpreglabridin
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: glabridin (15); demethylvestitol (13); preglabridin (14); corresponding demethylated products
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms glabridin and interconnects multiple routes by demethylating methyl-protected intermediates
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antioxidant flavonoid with skin-brightening, anti-inflammatory, and anti-aging properties
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Directly produces glabridin from 4′-O-methylglabridin and strongly redirects pathway flux through demethylation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GgDMT1 is a plant 2-oxoglutarate-dependent dioxygenase that demethylates 11 completely to glabridin. 2. It is highly promiscuous and also demethylates 9 and 10 efficiently. 3. Strong GgDMT1 activity collapses the ladder-like multi-route network into a less productive single-route pathway in yeast.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Co-expression prioritization; yeast feeding assays; purified protein in vitro assay; docking/mutational analysis; subcellular localization
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key branch-forming control step, but not the main bottleneck
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Galactose induction in assays; DDI2 cyanamide-inducible expression used to delay demethylation in de novo pathway
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Excessively strong demethylation increases flux to secreted demethylated intermediates, reducing glabridin yield
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Valuable for terminal glabridin formation, but expression timing should be delayed or moderated to maintain retention and multi-route robustness
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Represents a previously unreported plant isoflavonoid demethylase activity; N303 is critical for substrate positioning
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq: NCBI BioProject PRJNA1390709; metabolomics: MetaboLights MTBLS13519

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: GAL2 promoter in yeast pathway reconstruction
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Upstream tailoring step in heterologous route
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Prenylates medicarpin to licoagrocarpin in the alternative reconstructed route
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms alternative precursor for GgPTR2-dependent route to glabridin
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antioxidant flavonoid with skin-brightening, anti-inflammatory, and anti-aging properties
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enabled the PT-PTR-OC alternative route in yeast because no tested GgPT could catalyze 8 to 20
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PcM4DT catalyzed medicarpin to licoagrocarpin, unlike tested GgPTs. 2. It was used to reconstruct an alternative glabridin route with GgPTR2 and GgOC1. 3. This route confirmed network flexibility in yeast.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous pathway reconstruction in yeast; comparison with inactive GgPTs
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Galactose induction in yeast assays; DMAPP supply limitation influenced route performance
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Alternative route still suffered inefficient prenylation due to low DMAPP supply
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful heterologous enzyme for synthetic biology route diversification rather than native licorice breeding
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Background to licorice native pathway, but experimentally central to one validated reconstructed route

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: GAL2 promoter in yeast pathway reconstruction
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Dynamic methylation-demethylation cycle in multi-route network
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Demethylates multiple 4′-O-methylated isoflavonoid intermediates, including 5, 8, 9, 10, and 11
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: corresponding demethylated products including glabridin from 11
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Connects multiple routes and allows convergence to glabridin while preserving multi-route network behavior
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antioxidant flavonoid with skin-brightening, anti-inflammatory, and anti-aging properties
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Moderate demethylation preserved a productive ladder-like multi-route network and yielded more glabridin than the streamlined GgDMT1 route
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Among tested non-plant demethylases, only NhPDA1 was active on multiple methylated intermediates. 2. In yeast, NhPDA1 supported a multi-route ladder network close to the metabolite pattern observed in G. glabra. 3. This multi-route architecture gave higher glabridin yield than the simplified single-route design.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Demethylase screening; yeast pathway reconstruction; metabolite dynamics analysis
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Moderate activity can leave multiple intermediates distributed across the network
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful synthetic biology tool for maintaining distributed flux in engineered hosts
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used as engineering comparator to GgDMT1 rather than native licorice enzyme
